# Plan the warm-intro outreach batch

> **Spec maturity:** draft
> **Version:** 0.1.0
> **Linked files:** (none — personal workstream)

## Intent

**Job statement:** Plan and sequence a batch of warm-intro outreach conversations from my professional network — who to reach, in what order, and through which mutual connection — so the round of conversations over the next few weeks is deliberate rather than ad hoc.

This is a personal workstream, not an engineering task. I have a large network and a short list of the kinds of people the next few conversations should be with — a couple of target companies, a couple of roles, one or two specific names. The job is to turn that into an ordered plan: for each target, identify the warmest path in (a shared employer, a school overlap, a strong mutual), decide whether to ask the mutual for an intro or reach directly, and stage the asks so I am not leaning on the same well-connected person for five intros in one week. I want the sequence written down with a rough cadence — who this week, who next — and a note on the angle for each, so the outreach feels personal and considered on the receiving end. The output is a plan I can work down, not an automated send.

## Open Questions

- **[OPEN]** Whether to batch the asks to each well-connected mutual into a single message ("could you intro me to these two?") or keep them separate so each intro feels considered rather than transactional.
- **[OPEN]** How warm a connection has to be before a direct reach beats asking for an intro — the threshold is a judgment call I have not pinned down.
